name: pattern-violations
description: Handler that queries the DB directly (bypassing the service layer) and uses a default export — two house-pattern violations.

pr.title: Add orders endpoint
pr.author: dan
pr.description: Adds a GET /orders handler that returns the current user's orders.

agentResponse.summary: Two house-pattern violations found: the handler instantiates its own pg.Pool instead of using the shared getDbClient() helper, and it uses a default export where named exports are required.

findings.title: Bypasses the repository pattern, queries DB directly from handler
findings.detail: ordersHandler instantiates a raw pg.Pool inline and queries the database directly. All DB access must go through the shared getDbClient() helper; raw Pool construction is reserved for the db/client module only.

findings.title: Uses default export instead of the required named export
findings.detail: The handler is exported as `export default ordersHandler`. The house convention requires named exports for all modules to support tree-shaking and consistent import style across the codebase.

violatedPatterns: Always use shared getDbClient() for database access
Use named exports only — no default exports
